title: Metaball Lab
description: GPU-rendered metaballs with 4 shader modes — drag blobs, tune the field, watch organic forms merge and split.
concept: A WebGL fragment-shader playground where implicit surfaces (metaballs) are computed per-pixel on the GPU. Drag up to 16 blobs around a canvas; they merge and separate based on a shared scalar field. Four render modes (Neon, Heat, Plasma, Acid) apply completely different colorization. Negative-charge blobs subtract from the field, punching holes in the surface.
features: Click canvas to place up to 16 blobs; drag to reposition; double-click to remove
4 GPU render modes: Neon glow, Heat gradient, Plasma ripple, Acid bands
New-blob size slider + surface threshold slider for artistic control
Auto-animate: blobs bounce and merge continuously; toggle off to sculpt
Add Random Blob (occasionally negative-charge for holes)
Export current frame as PNG download
Full state (blobs, mode, threshold) saved to localStorage

interaction.touch: Tap to add, pinch not needed
